$schema: Every session MUST update this file as its LAST action. Never edit fields of units you did not work on.

legend.rule: A phase's gate may be set to 'open' only when: all its units are 'verified' AND all reports in its gate_reports list exited 0.

last_session.unit: U-V.1-U-V.4, U-R.1-U-R.6 (P4/P5 validation engine, out-of-order per explicit owner authorization)
last_session.note: OWNER AUTHORIZATION ON RECORD: P2's gate (U-B.4 sign-off) and P3's gate (7-day STATE_MACHINE_ENABLED=shadow soak) are closed only on human, time-based preconditions, not missing code; the owner explicitly authorized beginning P4/P5 implementation before those gates open, accepting that P4's own gate still cannot open until P2/P3 open regardless. ENGINE_MODE (and every other flag) stayed off/unset in production throughout -- zero production behavior change. Built the full validation-engine skeleton (U-V.1 value objects, U-V.2 TargetStateBuilder, U-V.3 ValidationEngine+ShadowRunner+the ServerBuilder::validateComponentAddition hook, U-V.4 parity_report.php) then ported all 6 first-wave rule families (U-R.1 cpu.*, U-R.2 memory.*, U-R.3 pcie.slot_placement+SlotPlanner, U-R.4 pcie.lane_budget, U-R.5 storage.*, U-R.6 net.*) -- 17 rules total, registered in ValidationEngine::RULES, each with a real-ims-data-fixture unit test (10 test files, all passing) and expected_diffs.json entries for every RULE_MAP-cited intentional divergence (A-2, A-8, A-9, A-10, A-12, D4). Extended ResourceCatalog (cpu_socket, dimm_slot, chassis drive-bay providers) beyond U-L.1's original scope, now authorized by U-R.1/U-R.2/U-R.5 read access to ComponentValidator/ComponentCompatibility. PD-2: value objects use PHP 7.4-compatible constants classes instead of the pack's literal 'PHP 8 enums' instruction (CLAUDE.md pins the stack at 7.4+; a parse error in an unconditionally-required file would break production regardless of flag state). All units implemented, NOT independently verified. U-R.7/U-R.8 remain not_started. See both handoff files for full per-unit detail, known gaps (storage.interface_path's SAS-backplane simplification, net.nic_requirements' honest SR-IOV gap), and the exact next-session prompt.
